cfg-d2q3.name: common_structure
cfg-d2q3.description: Shared common structure — cross-layer data structure with fields, functions, and validation

cfg-f7s8.name: data_type
cfg-f7s8.description: Shared data type — base type with constraints and serialization rules

cfg-h5v9.name: transformer
cfg-h5v9.description: Shared transformer — input-to-output type conversion with transformation rules

cfg-j9w2.name: shared_error_definitions
cfg-j9w2.description: Shared error type definitions — cross-layer error codes, categories, severity

cfg-k3x7.name: constant
cfg-k3x7.description: Shared constant — named value with type and category

cfg-l8y1.name: shared_settings

cfg-p4b8.description: Shared logging — cross-layer log format, levels, outputs, aggregation, and filters

cfg-q9c5.name: backend_logging
cfg-q9c5.description: Backend logging — processing layer log format, levels, and outputs

cfg-r3d7.name: frontend_logging

cfg-w4n3.name: documentation

chgprop-0001.description: Change propagation query — returns test file paths affected by a node change

comp-0002.description: Composed spec cache indexed by connected component

crawlstore-0001.description: SQLite-backed IN:DO:OUT crawl store — upsert atomicity, subgraph queries, staleness propagation, idempotent schema init

db-b7r2.description: Backend processor module — core data processing unit with operations

db-d3w8.name: data_access_object
db-d3w8.description: Data access object — encapsulates database queries and persistence

db-f8n5.name: data_structure
db-f8n5.description: Backend data structure — table/entity schema with fields, relations, and validations

db-h2s4.name: service
db-h2s4.description: Backend service — orchestrates data access objects and business logic

db-j6x9.name: backend_verifier
db-j6x9.description: Backend verifier — validation conditions applied to structures and operations

db-l1c3.name: backend_error_definitions
db-l1c3.description: Backend error type definitions — error codes, messages, categories

depval-0001.name: dependency_validation
depval-0001.description: Edge validation pre-check — determines if a proposed edge maintains DAG invariants

fs-x7p6.name: tla_artifact_store
fs-x7p6.description: Filesystem working directory for generated TLA+ specs (.tla), TLC configs (.cfg), and model-checker output logs.

fs-y3q2.name: plan_artifact_store
fs-y3q2.description: Filesystem location for implementation plan files (.md) that serve as input to refinement and generation pipelines. Activate if using plan-driven generation.

gwt-0001.name: closure_update
gwt-0001.when: the resource is added to the DAG
gwt-0001.then: transitive closure updates to include all reachable resources

gwt-0002.name: component_detection
gwt-0002.then: both appear in the same component

gwt-0003.name: context_query
gwt-0003.then: all transitive dependencies and their contracts are returned

gwt-0004.name: cycle_rejection
gwt-0004.given: an edge that would create a cycle is proposed
gwt-0004.then: the edge is rejected with an error

gwt-0008.name: state_var_translation
gwt-0008.given: a TLA+ spec with declared state variables
gwt-0008.when: the state variable translator processes it
gwt-0008.then: a data_structures artifact is produced with fields, types, and validation conforming to backend_schema

gwt-0009.name: action_translation
gwt-0009.given: a TLA+ spec with defined actions
gwt-0009.then: a processors.operations artifact is produced with parameters, returns, and error_types conforming to backend_schema

gwt-0010.name: invariant_translation
gwt-0010.then: verifiers and testing.assertions artifacts are produced conforming to backend and shared schemas

gwt-0011.name: trace_translation
gwt-0011.given: a TLC counterexample trace with state transitions
gwt-0011.then: concrete test scenarios are generated with setup, steps, and expected outcomes for each state

gwt-0012.name: reverse_closure_complete
gwt-0012.given: a registry DAG with nodes A→B→C (A depends on B depends on C)
gwt-0012.then: the result contains both A and B as affected nodes (full transitive reverse closure)

gwt-0013.name: leaf_has_no_impact
gwt-0013.given: a registry DAG where node X has no dependents (nothing points to X)
gwt-0013.then: the affected set is empty (leaf nodes have zero impact radius)

gwt-0014.name: direct_dependents_included
gwt-0014.given: a registry DAG where nodes D and E both directly depend on node F
gwt-0014.then: D and E both appear in the affected set and in direct_dependents

gwt-0015.name: valid_edge_accepted
gwt-0015.given: a registry DAG with compatible node kinds and no existing path from target to source
gwt-0015.then: validation passes with valid=True and empty reason

gwt-0016.name: cycle_creating_edge_rejected
gwt-0016.given: a registry DAG where node Y can already reach node X through existing edges
gwt-0016.when: validate_edge(X, Y, edge_type) is called (which would create a cycle)
gwt-0016.then: validation fails with valid=False and reason containing 'cycle'

gwt-0017.name: kind_incompatible_edge_rejected
gwt-0017.given: a registry DAG where a behavior node B exists and a test node T exists
gwt-0017.when: validate_edge(B, T, 'depends_on') is called (behavior depending on test)
gwt-0017.then: validation fails with valid=False and reason containing 'kind'

gwt-0018.name: chain_subgraph_complete
gwt-0018.given: a node in a chain (has ancestors and descendants)
gwt-0018.then: the result contains all ancestors AND all descendants of the node

gwt-0019.name: isolated_node_subgraph
gwt-0019.given: an isolated node with no edges
gwt-0019.then: the result contains only that node and no edges

gwt-0020.name: no_dangling_edges
gwt-0020.when: the edges in the result are inspected
gwt-0020.then: every edge has both endpoints in the node set (no dangling edges)

gwt-0021.name: upstream_change_propagates
gwt-0021.given: a node that a test-bearing node depends on
gwt-0021.then: the test path is in the result

gwt-0022.name: no_downstream_tests_empty
gwt-0022.given: a node with no downstream test artifacts

gwt-0023.name: self_test_included
gwt-0023.given: a node that IS a test-bearing node
gwt-0023.then: its own test path is included

gwt-0024.name: upsert_atomicity
gwt-0024.given: an existing record with UUID X and content C in crawl.db
gwt-0024.when: upsert_record is called with UUID X and updated content C'
gwt-0024.then: the old record and its ins/outs are replaced atomically: either all of (delete old, insert new) commits or none does, and no other connection observes a partial state

gwt-0025.name: forward_subgraph_complete
gwt-0025.given: a crawl.db with records A, B, C where A calls B and B calls C (via ins.source_uuid edges)
gwt-0025.then: the result contains A, B, and C (full transitive callee closure)

gwt-0026.name: staleness_propagation
gwt-0026.given: a crawl.db where record X has changed src_hash and record Y depends on X (Y has an ins with source_uuid = X.uuid)
gwt-0026.when: get_stale_records returns X as directly stale and get_transitive_stale is called with [X.uuid]
gwt-0026.then: Y appears in the transitive stale set (downstream dependents are marked stale)

gwt-0027.name: schema_init_idempotent
gwt-0027.given: a crawl.db that was previously initialized with the full schema
gwt-0027.when: connect() is called again (executescript runs CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS)
gwt-0027.then: the existing tables, indexes, and data are preserved unchanged — no data loss, no schema errors

gwt-0028.name: dfs_before_sweep
gwt-0028.given: a CrawlOrchestrator with entry points and pending records
gwt-0028.then: the DFS phase completes for all entry points before the sweep phase begins — no sweep extraction starts while DFS is still in progress

gwt-0029.name: graceful_shutdown_preserves_progress
gwt-0029.given: a CrawlOrchestrator mid-extraction (DFS or sweep phase active)
gwt-0029.when: SIGINT is received and _shutdown_requested is set to True
gwt-0029.then: any in-flight extract_one call completes and its result is persisted, but no new extractions are started — partial progress is preserved, not lost

gwt-0030.name: incremental_skip_unchanged
gwt-0030.given: a crawl.db with a previously-extracted record whose src_hash matches the current file content
gwt-0030.then: the record is skipped (returns True) and extract_fn is never invoked for that UUID

gwt-0031.name: semaphore_bounded_concurrency
gwt-0031.given: a sweep phase with N pending records and concurrency limit C
gwt-0031.then: at most C extract_one calls are active simultaneously — the semaphore prevents exceeding the concurrency bound

gwt-0032.name: extract_preserves_gwts
gwt-0032.given: a DAG file containing registered GWT and requirement nodes from prior cw9 register calls
gwt-0032.when: cw9 extract is run (SchemaExtractor.extract() builds a fresh DAG from schema files)
gwt-0032.then: all previously registered GWT and requirement nodes are preserved in the output DAG — extract merges schema-derived nodes with existing behavioral nodes rather than overwriting them

gwt-0033.name: loop_writes_spec_on_pass
gwt-0033.given: a GWT ID and a successful TLC verification (LoopResult.PASS) with a compiled spec at a temporary path
gwt-0033.then: the verified .tla file is copied to ctx.spec_dir/<gwt-id>.tla, the .cfg file is copied alongside it if present, and simulation traces are written to ctx.spec_dir/<gwt-id>_sim_traces.json

gwt-0034.name: pipeline_step_ordering
gwt-0034.given: a set of GWT IDs to process through cw9 pipeline
gwt-0034.when: cmd_pipeline runs the three phases (setup, loop, bridge)
gwt-0034.then: setup (init + extract) completes before any loop runs, all loop iterations complete before bridge begins, and bridge only processes GWTs that passed the loop phase — failures in setup cause early exit with nonzero return code

gwt-0035.name: register_idempotent
gwt-0035.given: a criterion_id that was previously registered (entry exists in .cw9/criterion_bindings.json)
gwt-0035.when: cw9 register is called again with the same criterion_id
gwt-0035.then: the previously allocated gwt_id is returned without calling dag.register_gwt() again — the bindings file provides stable ID allocation across invocations

gwt-0036.name: projectcontext_self_hosting_engine_root
gwt-0036.given: the engine_root directory contains templates/ and python/ subdirectories
gwt-0036.then: engine_root, target_root, and state_root all resolve to the same directory, and spec_dir is templates/pluscal/instances/ within that root

gwt-0037.name: projectcontext_from_target_target_root_i
gwt-0037.given: a target directory with .cw9/config.toml containing [engine].root pointing to a valid directory
gwt-0037.when: ProjectContext.from_target(target_root) is called without explicit engine_root
gwt-0037.then: engine_root is resolved from config.toml and the correct mode (self_hosting or external) is selected based on whether engine_root equals target_root

gwt-0038.name: projectcontext_external_engine_root_targ
gwt-0038.given: engine_root and target_root are different directories
gwt-0038.then: state_root is target_root/.cw9, schema_dir and spec_dir are under state_root, and template_dir and tools_dir come from engine_root

gwt-0039.name: build_retry_prompt_constructs_the_next_a
gwt-0039.given: a TLC verification attempt that fails with an identifiable error class (syntax_error, parse_error, type_error, invariant_violation, deadlock, constant_mismatch)
gwt-0039.when: build_retry_prompt constructs the next attempt prompt
gwt-0039.then: the retry prompt includes the classified error instruction block specific to that error class, the full previous LLM output, and the complete initial prompt

gwt-0040.name: the_test_generation_pipeline_executes_it
gwt-0040.given: bridge artifacts containing verifiers and a language profile
gwt-0040.when: the test generation pipeline executes its three passes (plan, review, codegen)
gwt-0040.then: each pass consumes the prior pass output in strict sequence, and the final code passes compile, collect, and run stages of verification

gwt-0041.name: the_test_plan_prompt_is_assembled
gwt-0041.given: a context bundle with simulation traces, API source, and GWT text
gwt-0041.when: the test plan prompt is assembled
gwt-0041.then: simulation traces appear first (rank 1), API context second (rank 2), verifiers and compiler hints third (rank 3), TLA+ spec text fourth (rank 4), and structural patterns last (rank 5)

gwt-0042.name: extract_db_path_is_called
gwt-0042.given: a CW7 SQLite database with a single session containing requirements and GWT-format acceptance criteria
gwt-0042.then: the output is a dict with requirements list (each having id and text) and gwts list (each having criterion_id of form cw7-crit-N, given, when, then, and parent_req)

gwt-0043.name: validate_depends_on_checks_the_authored
gwt-0043.given: research notes mentioning functions that exist in crawl.db and an LLM response containing depends_on UUIDs
gwt-0043.when: validate_depends_on checks the authored GWTs against crawl.db
gwt-0043.then: only UUIDs that exist in crawl.db records table are retained in depends_on, and invalid UUIDs are removed with warnings (not exceptions)

gwt-0044.name: bridge_crawl_to_dag_creates_resource_nod
gwt-0044.given: a crawl.db containing non-external function records with deterministic UUID5 identifiers
gwt-0044.when: bridge_crawl_to_dag creates RESOURCE nodes from crawl.db records
gwt-0044.then: every non-external record produces exactly one RESOURCE node in the DAG with id matching the record UUID, and the node count equals the record count

gwt-0045.name: the_orphan_cleanup_phase_runs_after_node
gwt-0045.given: a DAG containing RESOURCE nodes with UUID-format IDs and a crawl.db that no longer contains some of those UUIDs
gwt-0045.when: the orphan cleanup phase runs after node creation
gwt-0045.then: RESOURCE nodes with UUID-format IDs not found in crawl.db are removed from the DAG, while non-RESOURCE nodes and non-UUID-format nodes are preserved

impact-0001.name: impact_analysis
impact-0001.description: Reverse dependency query — finds all nodes transitively depending on a target

loop-0001.name: one_shot_loop
loop-0001.description: One-shot loop: registry context → LLM → PlusCal → compile → compose → TLC → route

loop-0002.name: counterexample_translator
loop-0002.description: Translates TLC counterexample traces to PlusCal-level concepts

loop-0003.name: pass_retry_fail_router
loop-0003.description: Routes TLC results: pass → done, first fail → retry, second fail → requirements inconsistency

mq-c1g5.name: interceptor_interface
mq-c1g5.description: Shared interceptor interface — contract defining interceptor method signatures

mq-r4z8.name: backend_process_chain
mq-r4z8.description: Backend process chain — ordered sequence of processor operations with conditions

mq-s9b4.name: interceptor
mq-s9b4.description: Middleware interceptor — hooks into execution stages with priority and conditions

mq-t2f7.name: execution_pattern
mq-t2f7.description: Middleware execution pattern — trigger conditions, rules, and bypass logic

mq-u6j3.name: middleware_process_chain
mq-u6j3.description: Middleware process chain — ordered interceptor chain with pre/post processing stages

orchestrator-0001.name: crawl_orchestrator
orchestrator-0001.description: DFS + concurrent sweep orchestrator — two-phase sequencing, graceful shutdown, incremental skip, semaphore-bounded concurrency

req-0001.text: System needs a dependency-tracking DAG over existing resource registry

req-0002.text: System needs a one-shot loop that queries registry context, prompts LLM, extracts PlusCal, compiles, composes, verifies with TLC, and routes pass/retry/fail

req-0003.text: System needs mechanical translators that convert TLA+ specs into code artifacts conforming to existing schema shapes (data_structures, processors.operations, verifiers, testing.assertions)

req-0004.text: System needs an impact analysis query: given a node, find all nodes that transitively depend on it (reverse closure), enabling change-impact assessment

req-0005.text: System needs an edge validation pre-check: given a proposed edge, determine whether adding it would maintain all DAG invariants (acyclicity, no duplicates, node-kind compatibility) without actually mutating the DAG

req-0006.text: System needs a subgraph extraction query: given a node, return its forward closure (descendants), reverse closure (ancestors), and all edges where both endpoints are in that set

req-0007.text: System needs a change propagation query: given a node, return the file paths of test files affected by a change to that node, using the impact set and a test_artifacts mapping

req-0008.text: CrawlStore needs formal invariants on its core operations: upsert atomicity, forward subgraph completeness, staleness propagation correctness, and schema initialization idempotency

req-0009.text: CrawlOrchestrator needs formal invariants on its core operations: DFS-before-sweep sequencing, graceful shutdown preservation, incremental skip correctness, and semaphore-bounded concurrency

req-0010.text: CLI pipeline (cw9 command) needs formal invariants on its core operations: extract preserving registered GWT nodes, loop persisting verified specs, pipeline step ordering with early exit, and register idempotency via criterion-ID bindings

req-0011.name: projectcontext_factory_methods_must_reso
req-0011.text: ProjectContext factory methods must resolve paths correctly for self-hosting, external, and installed modes

req-0012.name: llm_integration_must_classify_errors_for
req-0012.text: LLM integration must classify errors for targeted retry prompts and generate compilable tests through a multi-pass pipeline

req-0013.name: cw7_database_extraction_must_produce_reg
req-0013.text: CW7 database extraction must produce register-compatible JSON and GWT authoring must validate depends_on UUIDs against crawl.db

req-0014.name: crawl_bridge_must_synchronize_crawl.db_r
req-0014.text: Crawl bridge must synchronize crawl.db records to DAG RESOURCE nodes and remove orphaned nodes

res-0001.name: nodes
res-0001.description: Map<UUID, ResourceEntry> — the node map of the registry DAG

res-0002.name: edges
res-0002.description: List<{from, to, type}> — the edge list of the registry DAG

subgraph-0001.name: subgraph_extraction
subgraph-0001.description: Subgraph extraction query — returns minimal subgraph to understand a node

tpl-0001.name: crud_template
tpl-0001.description: CRUD PlusCal template — set-based state, create/read/update/delete actions, structural invariants

tpl-0002.name: state_machine_template
tpl-0002.description: State machine PlusCal template — enum states, guarded transitions, bypass conditions

tpl-0003.name: queue_pipeline_template
tpl-0003.description: Queue/pipeline PlusCal template — ordered processing, FIFO, no-item-loss invariant

tpl-0004.name: auth_session_template
tpl-0004.description: Auth/session PlusCal template — login/logout/expire, role-based access, session timeout

tpl-0005.name: registry_crud_spec
tpl-0005.description: Registry CRUD instantiation — verifies gwt-0001..0004 via TLC model checker

tpl-0006.name: composition_engine_spec
tpl-0006.description: State machine spec for the composition engine lifecycle (TLC verified)

tpl-0007.name: one_shot_loop_spec
tpl-0007.description: State machine spec for the one-shot loop lifecycle (TLC verified)

tpl-0008.name: bridge_translator_spec
tpl-0008.description: State machine spec for the bridge translator pipeline (TLC verified: 702 states, 6 invariants)

ui-a4y1.name: frontend_verifier
ui-a4y1.description: Frontend verifier — client-side validation rules applied to components

ui-v3n6.name: module
ui-v3n6.description: Frontend module — UI module with components, navigation, state, and events

ui-w8p2.name: component
ui-w8p2.description: Frontend component — UI element with properties, state, functions, event handlers, and templates

ui-x1r9.name: access_control
ui-x1r9.description: Frontend access control — route guard with condition and redirect

ui-y5t3.name: data_loader
ui-y5t3.description: Frontend data loader — async data fetcher bridging backend endpoints to UI

ui-z2w7.name: utility_module
ui-z2w7.description: Frontend utility module — reusable UI helper functions
